posts.caption:
Most teams do not have an AI problem. They have a handoff problem.

Work dies in the gaps between tools. A lead hits the CRM. Someone copies it into Slack. Another person rebuilds the same note in a spreadsheet. By the time a reply goes out, the moment has passed.

The automations that actually save money are boring. Capture the lead once. Enrich it. Route it. Draft the first reply. Log the outcome. No dashboards. No chatbot theater. Just fewer steps between interest and action.

If your week still depends on copy-paste between three apps, that is the first workflow worth fixing.

Where does work stall most often in your stack right now?

Follow me.
posts.title: Handoff problem not AI problem
